---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-banner}
# Basic R for Pharmacy

R for pharmaceutical sciences practicals.
Written for learners in plain language, tidyverse style, and with pharmacy examples.

[Get Started](getting-started.qmd){.hero-btn}
[Browse Functions](functions/index.qmd){.hero-btn .secondary}
:::


::: {.callout-tip}
## Run Code Online
Many code blocks on this site are **interactive**. Click **Run Code** to execute them right here in your browser — no R or RStudio installation needed.
:::

## Topics covered
<div class="card-grid">
<a href="getting-started.qmd" class="feature-card">
<div class="card-icon">🚀</div>
<h3>Getting Started</h3>
<p>Set up R and RStudio, and run your very first lines of code.</p>
</a>
<a href="topics/data-handling.qmd" class="feature-card">
<div class="card-icon">📂</div>
<h3>Data Handling</h3>
<p>Import spreadsheets, tidy messy data, and select the rows and columns you need.</p>
</a>
<a href="topics/visualization.qmd" class="feature-card">
<div class="card-icon">📈</div>
<h3>Visualization</h3>
<p>Create plots with ggplot2.</p>
</a>
<a href="topics/descriptive-stats.qmd" class="feature-card">
<div class="card-icon">📊</div>
<h3>Descriptive Statistics</h3>
<p>Summarise your data: means, medians, standard deviations, and more.</p>
</a>
<a href="#" class="feature-card" style="display:none">
<div class="card-icon">🔬</div>
<h3>Hypothesis Testing</h3>
<p>t-tests, chi-square, and Mann–Whitney tests for clinical data.</p>
</a>
<a href="#" class="feature-card" style="display:none">
<div class="card-icon">📐</div>
<h3>Regression</h3>
<p>Understand dose–response relationships with linear and logistic regression.</p>
</a>
</div>

---

## How to read a function page

| Section | What you'll find |
|---|---|
| **Required Library** | What library is needed to use the function |
| **Syntax** | How to use the function |
| **Argument Overview** | Relevant arguments explained |
| **Examples** | Examples for how to use the function with different arguments|
